summary.scholar: On March 1, 1961, President Kennedy established the Peace Corps by executive order, appointing Sargent Shriver as its first director. The program sent American volunteers abroad to assist developing nations.
summary.explorer: President Kennedy created the Peace Corps on March 1, 1961. This program sends American volunteers to other countries to help people in need.

significance.scholar: The Peace Corps became one of Kennedy most enduring legacies, inspiring generations of Americans to serve abroad.
significance.explorer: The Peace Corps is still going strong today! Thousands of Americans have traveled to other countries to help build schools, teach, and more.

sources.citation: John F. Kennedy: Life and Presidency
